# Feature Specification: ASI07 Tool-Abuse Enrichment (OWASP ASI07:2026)

**Feature Branch**: `219-asi07-tool-abuse-enrichment`
**Created**: 2026-04-25
**Status**: Draft
**Input**: User description: "PRD: 219 - asi07-tool-abuse-enrichment"
**PRD**: [docs/product/02_PRD/219-asi07-tool-abuse-enrichment-2026-04-25.md](../../docs/product/02_PRD/219-asi07-tool-abuse-enrichment-2026-04-25.md)
**BLP-01 Phase**: Tier 1 — third Tier 1 feature (parallel-eligible with F-4 and F-5; follows F-1 Feature 201 + F-2 Feature 206); first execution of Heuristic A **enrichment** branch (vs. new-agent branch validated by F-1 + F-2)

## User Scenarios & Testing *(mandatory)*

### User Story 1 — Inter-Agent Channel Detection (A2A) (Priority: P1)

A security analyst threat-models a multi-agent architecture where two or more agent Process components communicate over a channel — direct RPC, message bus, shared queue, MCP-to-MCP bridge, named pipe, or IPC — without declared mutual authentication, message signing, replay protection, or taint propagation. The analyst wants tachi to flag those channels with concrete `AG-{N}` findings citing OWASP ASI07:2026 so inter-agent spoofing, tampering, and replay surfaces are surfaced before they reach runtime, alongside the existing Categories 1-8 findings on individual agents.

**Why this priority**: This is the primary reason F-3 exists. Without inter-agent channel detection, the agentic threat surface remains asymmetric post-F-2: single-agent tool dispatch covered (Categories 1-4), MCP server poisoning covered (Categories 5-8), inter-agent channels silently uncovered. P1 because the feature has no MVP without this story.

**Independent Test**: Given an architecture containing ≥2 agent Process components connected by a communication channel without declared mutual authentication, running `/tachi.threat-model` emits ≥1 new `AG-{N}` finding with `category: agentic`, OWASP ASI07:2026 in `references`, and a populated `source_attribution` array. Given an architecture with exactly one agent (no inter-agent channel), the new Pattern Category 9 emits **zero findings** — Categories 1-8 fire as they do today.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an architecture with ≥2 agent Process components connected by a communication channel without declared mutual authentication, **when** the orchestrator dispatches `tool-abuse`, **then** an `AG-{N}` finding emits with `category: agentic`, `references` citing `OWASP ASI07:2026`, and `source_attribution` containing `{taxonomy: owasp, id: ASI07, relationship: primary}` plus `{taxonomy: cwe, id: CWE-287, relationship: related}`.
2. **Given** inter-agent messages without HMAC envelope signatures, timestamp binding, or replay-window enforcement, **when** `tool-abuse` runs, **then** distinct `AG-{N}` findings surface the message-integrity gap with named mitigations (HMAC message signing with per-call nonce, timestamp binding, replay-window enforcement).
3. **Given** an agent that acts as a message relay between two other agents without declared taint propagation (the relay's outputs do not carry the upstream sender's authority labels), **when** `tool-abuse` runs, **then** a finding highlights the agent-in-the-middle surface and (where catalog-resolvable) cites `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0060` in `source_attribution` as `relationship: related`.
4. **Given** an architecture with exactly one agent (no inter-agent channel), **when** `tool-abuse` runs, **then** Pattern Category 9 emits **zero findings** — the existing tool-abuse Categories 1-8 fire as they do today; the new Category 9 is gated on multi-agent topology and does not speculate on hypothetical second agents.
5. **Given** an architecture exercising both single-agent tool dispatch AND inter-agent communication channels, **when** `/tachi.threat-model` runs, **then** Categories 1-8 findings AND new Category 9 findings render in the same `category: agentic` section of `threats.md` and `threat-report.md` with sequential `AG-{N}` IDs — single-namespace ID space, no fragmentation across artificial categories.

---

### User Story 2 — MCP-to-MCP Trust Propagation Detection (Priority: P1)

A developer operates a multi-MCP agent fleet where Agent → MCP-A → MCP-B forms a multi-hop trust chain. The developer wants tachi to surface MCP-to-MCP trust-propagation risks distinct from single-agent MCP poisoning (Categories 5-8 cover MCP server endpoints; Category 10 covers the **bridge** between two or more MCP servers) so the cross-MCP trust boundary can be hardened with concrete per-hop attestation, signed-capability handoff, and trust-chain validator mechanisms.

**Why this priority**: Equal priority to Story 1 because MCP-to-MCP trust propagation is a structurally distinct surface from A2A inter-agent communication — same Heuristic A signal class (message flow between agent-or-tool endpoints) but a different deployment topology. Equal P1 because adopters running multi-MCP fleets need per-hop attestation guidance whether or not their architecture also exhibits A2A channels.

**Independent Test**: Given an architecture where an agent dispatches to a remote MCP server which in turn dispatches to a secondary MCP server without declared trust-chain attestation, running `/tachi.threat-model` emits ≥1 new `AG-{N}` finding citing OWASP ASI07:2026 primary + CWE-345 related + (where applicable) OWASP LLM03:2025 related. Given an architecture with exactly one MCP server (no MCP-to-MCP relay), Pattern Category 10 emits zero findings — single-MCP architectures continue to receive Categories 5-8 findings as they do today.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an architecture where an agent dispatches to MCP-A which in turn dispatches to MCP-B without declared trust-chain attestation, **when** `tool-abuse` runs, **then** an `AG-{N}` finding emits with primary citation `OWASP ASI07:2026` and `source_attribution` containing `{taxonomy: owasp, id: ASI07, relationship: primary}` plus `{taxonomy: cwe, id: CWE-345, relationship: related}`. Where the upstream-trust reasoning is surfaced in the threat description, `{taxonomy: owasp, id: LLM03, relationship: related}` MAY appear (inheriting the supply-chain vocabulary established by existing Category 6).
2. **Given** an `AG-{N}` finding for the MCP-to-MCP trust propagation category, **when** the `mitigation` field is read, **then** it names at least one concrete control: per-hop MCP attestation, signed-capability handoff, MCP-trust-chain validator, supply-chain trust-chain enforcement.
3. **Given** an architecture with exactly one MCP server (no MCP-to-MCP relay), **when** `tool-abuse` runs, **then** Pattern Category 10 emits **zero findings** — single-MCP architectures continue to receive Categories 5-8 findings as they do today.
4. **Given** an architecture where MCP-A is unsigned at registration (existing Category 6 supply-chain) AND MCP-A relays to MCP-B without per-hop attestation (new Category 10 trust propagation), **when** `tool-abuse` runs, **then** **both** findings emit — they are not duplicates and MUST NOT be merged in the threat-report's Agentic-category section. The Pattern Category Disambiguation contract carves Category 6 (upstream ingestion of plugins/tools/MCP servers at registration time) from Category 10 (runtime trust propagation between already-registered MCP servers at invocation time).

---

### User Story 3 — Cohesive Agentic-Category Rendering with Heuristic A Enrichment Pattern (Priority: P1)

An adopter of a multi-agent canonical example (MAESTRO CDSS reference, `examples/agentic-app/` per Feature 142, or any extended multi-agent topology) runs `/tachi.threat-model`. The adopter wants existing tool-abuse findings (Categories 1-8) plus the new ASI07 findings (Categories 9-10) rendered cohesively in the same `category: agentic` section of `threats.md` and `threat-report.md` so the report does not fragment agentic threats across two artificial categories that would emerge if ASI07 were a new agent. The Heuristic A consolidation rationale is documented in public ADR-032 (Proposed → Accepted dual-commit pattern per ADR-027/028/029/030/031 precedent) so the enrichment-vs-new-agent decision is auditable.

**Why this priority**: Equal priority to Stories 1 and 2 because the cohesive-rendering discipline gates both (a) the adopter-facing report coherence on architectures exercising multiple agentic surfaces simultaneously and (b) the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix transition from ASI07:2026 Planned → Covered. Without Heuristic A enrichment validation, the consolidation pattern remains untested in production and future ASI06/ASI08 enrichments would lack precedent.

**Independent Test**: Public ADR-032 at F-3 merge contains an explicit Heuristic A enrichment-rationale section resolving (a) signal-class identity with existing tool-abuse coverage (message flow between agent-or-tool endpoints), (b) the explicit non-creation of a new agent and rationale (splitting would fragment the Agentic-category section without semantic gain), (c) cross-reference to ADR-030 Decision 1 (signal-class taxonomy in LLM tier as a different application of the same rule), (d) cross-reference to ADR-031 Decision 8 as the **asymmetry** (regex-alternation minor-bump rule applies to F-1/F-2 but NOT F-3 — Heuristic A consolidation reuses the existing host's ID space). The regenerated example architecture renders Categories 1-8 findings AND Categories 9-10 findings adjacent in the same `category: agentic` section.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** a regenerated example architecture exercising both single-agent tool dispatch AND inter-agent communication channels, **when** the AI-category section of `threat-report.md` is read, **then** all `AG-{N}` findings render in the same `category: agentic` section regardless of which Pattern Category (1-10) produced them. The ID prefix `AG-{N}` is **single-namespace** — sequential numbering across all 10 categories.
2. **Given** ADR-032 at the time of F-3 merge, **when** its Decision section is read, **then** it explicitly resolves the Heuristic A consolidation rationale: (a) ASI07 signal class is identical to existing tool-abuse coverage, (b) creating a new agent would fragment the Agentic-category section without semantic gain, (c) the additive-only edit discipline preserves byte-identity on existing categories per ADR-023 Decision 3, (d) the public ADR omits commercial framing and SDR-001 cross-reference per Option C governance contract.
3. **Given** the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix post-F-3 merge, **when** ASI07:2026 row is inspected, **then** it transitions **Planned → Covered** with F-3 (Feature 219) named as the closure feature.
4. **Given** the Coverage Matrix post-F-3 merge, **when** the OWASP Agentic Top 10:2026 framework row is summed, **then** ASI07 joins ASI-01 / ASI-02 / ASI-04 / MCP-03 / MCP-05 as the sixth Agentic Top 10 entry covered (out of 10), with F-4 (ASI09) and post-Tier-1 features tracking the remaining 4.
5. **Given** ADR-032 lands as Proposed before pattern catalog edits and transitions to Accepted at PR merge, **when** the ADR Revision History table is inspected, **then** it tracks Proposed → Accepted dates and the post-merge SHA fill records the squash commit (mirrors ADR-027/028/029/030/031 precedent).

---

### Edge Cases

- **Single-agent architecture (no inter-agent channel)**: Pattern Category 9 emits zero findings — the indicator predicate "≥2 agent Process components connected by a communication channel" fails on architectures with one agent. Categories 1-8 continue to fire as they do today on the single agent's tool dispatch. No false positives on single-agent topologies.
- **Single-MCP architecture (no MCP-to-MCP relay)**: Pattern Category 10 emits zero findings — the indicator predicate "agent dispatches to remote MCP server which in turn dispatches to a secondary MCP server" fails on architectures with one MCP. Categories 5-8 continue to fire as they do today on the single MCP server. No false positives on single-MCP topologies.
- **Architecture with both Category 6 (LLM03 supply-chain) AND Category 10 (MCP-to-MCP) signals**: BOTH findings emit. They are not duplicates — Category 6 fires on **upstream ingestion** of plugins/tools/MCP servers (sourcing, registration, manifest pinning at registry time); Category 10 fires on **runtime trust propagation** between already-registered MCP servers (per-hop attestation, signed-capability handoff at invocation time). The same architecture may legitimately surface both findings describing distinct architectural gaps. Architect to formalize this carve in ADR-032 (likely as Decision 7 — Pattern Category Disambiguation).
- **5 non-multi-agent baselines (`web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`)**: do not exhibit multi-agent inter-agent channels and do not exhibit MCP-to-MCP relays. Categories 9 and 10 emit zero findings on all 5 baselines. All 5 PDFs regenerate **byte-identically** under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021. The 6th baseline (`agentic-app`) is the expected mutation candidate per FR-9.
- **`mermaid-agentic-app` baseline classification**: stylistic agentic multi-agent coordination without declared inter-agent communication channels. PRD-time architect inspection treats this baseline as zero-impact under Categories 9-10. Plan stage re-verifies — if `mermaid-agentic-app` unexpectedly triggers an emission, the plan stage MUST surface a re-baseline decision with explicit architect + team-lead approval.
- **F-A2 referential validation failure on absent catalog IDs**: every Category-9/10 finding's `source_attribution` MUST cite only IDs verified present in `schemas/taxonomy/{owasp,cwe,mitre-atlas}.yaml`. PRD-time verification confirms ASI07, CWE-287, CWE-345, AML.T0060, LLM03 are all catalog-resolvable; F-A2 referential-integrity validator passes by construction.
- **Categories 1-8 byte-identity preservation**: the additive-only edit discipline per ADR-023 Decision 3 requires that Categories 1-8 in `detection-patterns.md` remain byte-identical pre/post edit. A grep-checkable invariant — a structural diff of pre/post content for Categories 1-8 returns empty.
- **Existing `## Purpose` prose preservation**: the 1-3 line extension to `tool-abuse.md` `## Purpose` is appended; the pre-existing prose paragraphs remain byte-identical. A structural diff of `## Purpose` content excluding the appended lines returns empty.
- **Line count cap**: `tool-abuse.md` post-edit MUST remain ≤150 lines. PRD-time baseline 98 lines; expected post-edit 100-106 lines. The 95% margin reserves capacity for future enrichment without re-architecture.
- **Agent-in-the-middle relay topology with no taint propagation**: an agent that acts as a relay between two other agents without declared taint propagation is the canonical AML.T0060 trigger. Category 9 indicator "An agent acts as a message relay between two other agents without declared taint propagation" captures this; the finding cites `AML.T0060` in `source_attribution` as `relationship: related`.
- **Concurrent F-3 + F-4 + F-5 build-time conflict on `tool-abuse.md`**: F-3 has the smallest edit surface among Tier 1 features (2 host files only; no shared-edit surfaces with F-4/F-5). If F-4 (ASI09) or F-5 (LLM10) lands `tool-abuse.md` edits concurrently, sequencing F-3 first minimizes rebase friction. Buffer Day 2 (2026-04-30) absorbs concurrency-rebase work. Plan stage re-verifies the calendar at build start.
- **Cosmetic dispatch-rules annotation carve-out (Q2)**: extending `tool-abuse (MCP-03)` to `tool-abuse (MCP-03, ASI-07)` in `dispatch-rules.md` is documentation-only — no functional dispatch change. Architect adjudicates at plan time per PRD Q2 (architect leaning YES). If applied, the edit is a single-token annotation update; the zero-functional-touch claim on the orchestrator/dispatch tier is preserved.

---

## Requirements *(mandatory)*

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: The system MUST extend the metadata YAML in `.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md` with `ASI-07` appended to the `owasp_references` list — pre-edit `[ASI-02, ASI-04, MCP-03, MCP-05, LLM06:2025]` becomes `[ASI-02, ASI-04, MCP-03, MCP-05, LLM06:2025, ASI-07]`. Existing entries MUST be preserved byte-identically (grep-checkable). The agent file post-edit line count MUST remain ≤150 (AI tier cap per ADR-023).
- **FR-002**: The system MUST extend the `## Purpose` section in `.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md` with a 1-3 line extension naming the A2A / MCP-to-MCP surface alongside the existing tool-dispatch surface. The pre-existing `## Purpose` prose MUST remain byte-identical (the edit is additive append, not rewrite).
- **FR-003**: The system MUST extend the Detection Workflow Step 5 references list in `.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md` with `ASI-07`, `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0060`, `CWE-287`, and `CWE-345`. Existing references MUST be preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-004**: The system MUST append two new Pattern Categories to `.claude/skills/tachi-tool-abuse/references/detection-patterns.md` after the existing Pattern Category 8: **Pattern Category 9 — Insecure Inter-Agent Communication (A2A)** and **Pattern Category 10 — MCP-to-MCP Trust Propagation**. Existing Pattern Categories 1-8 and the file's `## Overview`, `## Targeted DFD Element Types`, `## Trigger Keywords` sections MUST remain byte-identical pre/post edit per ADR-023 Decision 3 (grep-checkable additive-only invariant).
- **FR-005**: Pattern Category 9 MUST carry: (a) ≥4 indicators covering ≥2 agents connected by a communication channel, absence of mutual authentication, absence of message signing, absence of replay protection, and agent-in-the-middle relay without taint propagation; (b) ≥1 worked example demonstrating an A2A channel surface; (c) primary-source citation `OWASP ASI07:2026 — Insecure Inter-Agent Communication`; (d) related-source citations `CWE-287 Improper Authentication` and (where applicable) `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0060 — Agent-in-the-Middle`; (e) named mitigations including mTLS, HMAC message signing, nonce-based replay prevention, taint propagation, per-channel mutual authentication.
- **FR-006**: Pattern Category 10 MUST carry: (a) ≥4 indicators covering multi-hop MCP trust chain, absence of per-hop attestation, transitive authority assumption gaps, absence of trust-chain validator, undeclared cross-MCP supply-chain assumptions; (b) ≥1 worked example demonstrating an MCP-to-MCP trust-propagation surface; (c) primary-source citation `OWASP ASI07:2026 — Insecure Inter-Agent Communication`; (d) related-source citations `CWE-345 Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity` and (where applicable) `OWASP LLM03:2025 — Supply Chain` (inherited from existing Category 6 supply-chain vocabulary); (e) named mitigations including per-hop MCP attestation, signed-capability handoff, MCP-trust-chain validator, supply-chain trust-chain enforcement, taint propagation across MCP hops.
- **FR-007**: The system MUST extend the `## Primary Sources` list in `.claude/skills/tachi-tool-abuse/references/detection-patterns.md` with two additive entries: `OWASP ASI07:2026 — Insecure Inter-Agent Communication` and `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0060 — Agent-in-the-Middle`. Existing Primary Sources entries MUST be preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-008**: The system MUST ship a public per-feature ADR at `docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-NNN-asi07-tool-abuse-enrichment.md` (NNN expected to be 032; team-lead confirms numbering at plan time) under the Proposed → Accepted dual-commit pattern (ADR-027/028/029/030/031 precedent). ADR body MUST include: (a) the Heuristic A enrichment decision with rationale (signal-class identity with existing tool-abuse coverage; consolidation prevents Agentic-category section fragmentation), (b) the additive-only edit discipline per ADR-023 Decision 3 with byte-identity proof on Categories 1-8, (c) the explicit non-creation of a new agent, (d) zero-MAESTRO-reference invariant proof on the enriched files, (e) cross-references to ADR-021 (byte-identity baseline harness), ADR-023 (lean+skill-references pattern, additive-only edits), ADR-027 (taxonomy crosswalk), ADR-028 (source attribution schema), ADR-030 Decision 1 (signal-class taxonomy in LLM tier as different application of same rule), ADR-031 Decision 8 (regex-alternation minor-bump rule as the asymmetry F-3 does not invoke), (f) Revision History table tracking Proposed → Accepted dates with post-merge SHA fill, (g) no Layer 2 / tachi Cloud / commercial framing — public ADR omits SDR-001 cross-reference per Option C governance contract.
- **FR-009**: The system MUST regenerate at least one example architecture that exercises the new flow. PM default per PRD Q3: extend `examples/agentic-app/` (multi-agent topology established by Feature 142). Architect MAY override at plan time with `examples/maestro-reference/` (canonical MAESTRO CDSS reference) or a newly-introduced minimal multi-agent fixture (consuming buffer day capacity) if `examples/agentic-app/` does not exhibit a sufficiently clean inter-agent channel signal. The regenerated example MUST surface ≥1 new `AG-{N}` finding attributable to Pattern Category 9 OR 10 with concrete inter-agent / MCP-trust mitigations, OWASP ASI07:2026 citation, and populated `source_attribution`.
- **FR-010**: The agent file `.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md` and its companion `.claude/skills/tachi-tool-abuse/references/detection-patterns.md` MUST contain **zero MAESTRO references** post-edit — grep-checkable invariant per ADR-023 Decision 2. PRD-time baseline: zero MAESTRO references in both files.
- **FR-011**: The two new Pattern Categories MUST enforce a **multi-agent / multi-MCP topology gate**: Category 9 emits findings only when ≥2 agent Process components are connected by a communication channel; Category 10 emits findings only when an architecture exhibits a multi-hop MCP trust chain (Agent → MCP-A → MCP-B). Architectures with one agent or one MCP server MUST emit **zero** Category-9/10 findings — Categories 1-8 fire as they do today. Matches the zero-speculation discipline established by F-1 FR-011 and F-2 FR-011.
- **FR-012**: Orchestrator Phase 4 `validate_source_attribution` MUST resolve every `source_attribution` record on every Category-9/10 `AG-{N}` finding without error. Pattern catalog worked examples and source-attribution citations MUST cite only taxonomy IDs verified present in F-A1 catalogs (`schemas/taxonomy/owasp.yaml`, `schemas/taxonomy/cwe.yaml`, `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-atlas.yaml`). PRD-time verification: ASI07, CWE-287, CWE-345, AML.T0060, LLM03 all present; F-A2 validator passes by construction.
- **FR-013**: The system MUST NOT bump `schemas/finding.yaml`. Schema version remains `"1.7"` post-F-3. The `id.pattern` regex remains `^(S|T|R|I|D|E|AG|LLM|AGP|OI|MI)-\d+$` — no new prefix; `AG` already enumerated. F-3 is the **first BLP-01 detection feature with no schema bump** — Heuristic A consolidation reuses the existing host's ID space.
- **FR-014**: The system MUST NOT edit `.claude/skills/tachi-shared/references/finding-format-shared.md`. PRD-time verification: `tool-abuse` is already at line 18 in the consumers list. F-3 reuses the existing consumer registration.
- **FR-015**: The system MUST NOT edit any of the **24 detection-tier files** other than the host agent + companion catalog. The 24 files are: 12 other threat-agent files at `.claude/agents/tachi/{spoofing,tampering,repudiation,info-disclosure,denial-of-service,privilege-escalation,prompt-injection,data-poisoning,model-theft,agent-autonomy,output-integrity,misinformation}.md` plus their 12 companion `detection-patterns.md` references under `.claude/skills/tachi-{spoofing,tampering,repudiation,info-disclosure,denial-of-service,privilege-escalation,prompt-injection,data-poisoning,model-theft,agent-autonomy,output-integrity,misinformation}/references/`. Grep-auditable zero-edit invariant (extended count post-F-1 + F-2: 26 detection files; F-3 edits 2 host files; the remaining 24 stay byte-identical).
- **FR-016**: The system MUST NOT functionally edit the orchestrator dispatch tier. PRD-time verification: `tool-abuse` is already registered across multiple callsites in `.claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md` and `.claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/references/dispatch-rules.md`. **Annotation carve-out**: a cosmetic one-token annotation update to `dispatch-rules.md` (extending `tool-abuse (MCP-03)` to `tool-abuse (MCP-03, ASI-07)`) is permitted contingent on architect adjudication of PRD Q2 at plan time — this is documentation-only, no functional dispatch change, and does not invalidate the zero-functional-touch claim. F-3 is the **first BLP-01 detection feature with zero functional orchestrator-tier touches**.
- **FR-017**: The system MUST NOT edit infrastructure-tier consumer agents (`risk-scorer`, `control-analyzer`, `threat-report`, `threat-infographic`, `report-assembler`). These read `category: agentic` (existing enum value); new Category-9/10 findings flow through them without consumer-side changes using the same code paths that already process Categories 1-8 findings.
- **FR-018**: The system MUST NOT add new runtime dependencies — `pyproject.toml`, `requirements*.txt`, `package.json` diffs MUST be empty. No new developer dependencies (`pyyaml`, `pytest` already declared per Feature 128).
- **FR-019**: ADR-032 MUST land as Proposed at the start of the build phase to lock the Heuristic A enrichment decision before pattern-catalog authoring begins; MUST transition Accepted at PR merge with provisional merge-date; MUST post-merge SHA fill recording the squash commit (ADR-027/028/029/030/031 precedent). Plan stage finalizes the exact wave/slot for the Proposed commit.
- **FR-020**: F-3 is the **third net-new producer of `source_attribution`** in the tachi codebase post-F-A2. The existing `tool-abuse` agent already populates `source_attribution` on Categories 1-8 findings (extended in prior wiring work) — F-3 extends an existing populator with two new categories rather than adding a brand-new populator agent. This validates the F-A2 referential-integrity contract across **two agent classes** (LLM new — F-1 OI + F-2 MI — plus AG enrichment — F-3) in addition to the two-agent-new validation already established by F-1 + F-2.
- **FR-021**: The PR title MUST conform to Conventional Commits with `feat(219):` prefix so release-please fires on merge. Recommended title (architect/team-lead may refine at plan time): `feat(219): asi07 inter-agent communication tool-abuse enrichment` or similar Conventional Commit form ≤70 chars. The `/aod.deliver` stage re-verifies pre-merge and recovers via empty release-marker commit if release-please skips post-merge per F-212 incident precedent.

### Key Entities

- **`tool-abuse` agent** (`.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md`) — existing AI-tier threat detection agent; F-3 enriches metadata, `## Purpose`, and Detection Workflow Step 5 references. Outputs ordered list of `AG-{N}` findings per F-A2 schema.
- **`tachi-tool-abuse` skill** (`.claude/skills/tachi-tool-abuse/`) — existing companion skill directory. F-3 enriches `references/detection-patterns.md` with two new Pattern Categories (9 + 10) and two new Primary Sources entries.
- **`AG-{N}` finding** — existing finding-ID prefix for agentic findings. Single-namespace ID space across all 10 categories (1-8 existing + 9-10 new); sequential numbering.
- **Pattern Category 9 — Insecure Inter-Agent Communication (A2A)** — new pattern category in `detection-patterns.md` covering A2A communication channels (direct RPC, message bus, shared queue, MCP-to-MCP bridge, named pipe, IPC) without declared mutual authentication, message signing, replay protection, or taint propagation. Indicators ≥4. Primary: OWASP ASI07:2026. Related: CWE-287, AML.T0060.
- **Pattern Category 10 — MCP-to-MCP Trust Propagation** — new pattern category covering multi-hop MCP trust chains (Agent → MCP-A → MCP-B) without per-hop attestation, signed-capability handoff, or trust-chain validator. Indicators ≥4. Primary: OWASP ASI07:2026. Related: CWE-345, LLM03:2025 (inherited supply-chain vocabulary).
- **ADR-032** (`docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-NNN-asi07-tool-abuse-enrichment.md`) — public per-feature ADR documenting the Heuristic A enrichment pattern as the operational precedent. Authored under dual-commit Proposed → Accepted protocol. Public-only; SDR-001 cross-reference lives in private companion docs per Option C governance.
- **Multi-agent / multi-MCP topology gate** — detection workflow invariant requiring ≥2 agent Process components connected by a channel (Category 9) or a multi-hop MCP trust chain (Category 10) before finding emission. Single-agent and single-MCP architectures emit zero new Category-9/10 findings.
- **Pattern Category Disambiguation** — explicit non-overlap contract between Category 6 (LLM03 supply-chain — upstream ingestion at registration time) and Category 10 (MCP-to-MCP trust propagation — runtime trust between already-registered MCP servers). Documented in ADR-032 Decision 7 (architect-finalized at plan time).

---

## Success Criteria *(mandatory)*

### Measurable Outcomes

- **SC-001**: `.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md` `owasp_references` list includes `ASI-07`; pre-existing entries (`ASI-02, ASI-04, MCP-03, MCP-05, LLM06:2025`) are preserved byte-identically. Verifiable via grep against the metadata YAML block.
- **SC-002**: `.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md` post-edit line count is **≤150 lines** (AI tier cap per ADR-023); PRD-time baseline 98 lines; expected post-edit 100-106 lines. Verifiable via `wc -l`.
- **SC-003**: `.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md` `## Purpose` section includes a 1-3 line extension naming the A2A / MCP-to-MCP surface; the pre-existing `## Purpose` prose paragraph is byte-identical to its PRD-time state. Verifiable via structural diff of the section excluding the appended lines.
- **SC-004**: `.claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md` Detection Workflow Step 5 references list includes `ASI-07`, `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0060`, `CWE-287`, and `CWE-345`; pre-existing entries are preserved byte-identically. Verifiable via grep against Step 5.
- **SC-005**: `.claude/skills/tachi-tool-abuse/references/detection-patterns.md` ships **Pattern Category 9 — Insecure Inter-Agent Communication (A2A)** and **Pattern Category 10 — MCP-to-MCP Trust Propagation** appended after Category 8. Each new category carries ≥4 indicators, ≥1 worked example, and named mitigations. Verifiable via section-header grep + per-category content inspection.
- **SC-006**: `.claude/skills/tachi-tool-abuse/references/detection-patterns.md` Pattern Categories 1-8 plus the file's `## Overview`, `## Targeted DFD Element Types`, and `## Trigger Keywords` sections are **byte-identical pre/post edit** per ADR-023 Decision 3. Verifiable via structural diff returning empty for the unchanged regions (additive-only invariant).
- **SC-007**: `.claude/skills/tachi-tool-abuse/references/detection-patterns.md` `## Primary Sources` list includes `OWASP ASI07:2026 — Insecure Inter-Agent Communication` and `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0060 — Agent-in-the-Middle`; existing entries are preserved byte-identically.
- **SC-008**: Public ADR-032 (or next-available ADR number assigned at plan time) is committed at `docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-NNN-asi07-tool-abuse-enrichment.md` documenting the Heuristic A enrichment decision, additive-only edit discipline conformance, explicit non-creation of a new agent, zero-MAESTRO-reference invariant proof, and ADR cross-references (ADR-021, ADR-023, ADR-027, ADR-028, ADR-030 Decision 1, ADR-031 Decision 8). Authored under dual-commit Proposed → Accepted pattern with Revision History table tracking dates and post-merge SHA fill.
- **SC-009**: Orchestrator dispatch invokes `tool-abuse` on architectures matching the existing AG dispatch trigger keywords (no change to dispatch path). The two-part topology gate (FR-011) ensures Categories 9-10 emit ≥1 new `AG-{N}` finding on the regenerated multi-agent example architecture. Architectures with one agent or one MCP server emit zero new Category-9/10 findings — the agent self-gates emission to prevent speculation.
- **SC-010**: All **5 non-multi-agent example PDF baselines** (`web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`) regenerate **byte-identically** under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021 (zero-impact-when-absent invariant — the topology gate guarantees zero Category-9/10 findings on these baselines). The 6th baseline (`agentic-app` or architect-chosen multi-agent example) is the expected mutation candidate per FR-9.
- **SC-011**: Example regeneration lands on a multi-agent architecture (PM default per PRD Q3: extend `examples/agentic-app/`; architect may override at plan time with `examples/maestro-reference/` or a new minimal multi-agent fixture) and surfaces ≥1 new `AG-{N}` finding attributable to Pattern Category 9 OR 10 with concrete mitigations and OWASP ASI07:2026 citation.
- **SC-012**: Zero new runtime dependencies — empty diff on `pyproject.toml`, `requirements*.txt`, `package.json`. Zero new developer dependencies.
- **SC-013**: **24-file zero-edit invariant preserved** on every detection-tier file other than the host agent + companion catalog. Grep-auditable: zero edits to the 12 other threat-agent files at `.claude/agents/tachi/{spoofing,tampering,repudiation,info-disclosure,denial-of-service,privilege-escalation,prompt-injection,data-poisoning,model-theft,agent-autonomy,output-integrity,misinformation}.md` or their 12 companion `detection-patterns.md` reference files.
- **SC-014**: **Schema invariant preserved** — `schemas/finding.yaml` `schema_version` remains `"1.7"` post-F-3. The `id.pattern` regex remains unchanged. F-3 is the first BLP-01 detection feature with no schema bump. Verifiable via empty diff on `schemas/finding.yaml`.
- **SC-015**: Every emitted Category-9/10 `AG-{N}` finding passes F-A2 referential-integrity validation (`validate_source_attribution` returns no errors). Every Category-9 finding carries at minimum `{taxonomy: owasp, id: ASI07, relationship: primary}` plus `{taxonomy: cwe, id: CWE-287, relationship: related}`; every Category-10 finding carries at minimum `{taxonomy: owasp, id: ASI07, relationship: primary}` plus `{taxonomy: cwe, id: CWE-345, relationship: related}`. F-3 is the third net-new producer of `source_attribution` post-F-A2 (after F-1 OI-{N} and F-2 MI-{N}); the contract is now validated across two new-agent producers (LLM tier) AND one enrichment producer (AG tier).
- **SC-016**: The agent file and its companion `detection-patterns.md` contain **zero MAESTRO references** — verified via `grep -i 'maestro' .claude/agents/tachi/tool-abuse.md .claude/skills/tachi-tool-abuse/references/detection-patterns.md` returning no matches. PRD-time baseline: zero MAESTRO references in both files; F-3 preserves this invariant.
- **SC-017**: `.claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md` and `.claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/references/dispatch-rules.md` receive **zero functional edits** in F-3. An optional cosmetic annotation carve-out (extending `tool-abuse (MCP-03)` to `tool-abuse (MCP-03, ASI-07)` in `dispatch-rules.md` per PRD Q2) is permitted contingent on architect plan-day adjudication; if applied, it is documentation-only and does not constitute a functional dispatch change.
- **SC-018**: BLP-01 Coverage Matrix updated post-merge: ASI07:2026 transitions Planned → Covered with F-3 (Feature 219) named as the closure feature. OWASP Agentic Top 10:2026 framework coverage advances from 5/10 (post-F-2) to 6/10 (post-F-3) — ASI07 joins ASI-01, ASI-02, ASI-04, MCP-03, MCP-05.
- **SC-019**: Cohesive Agentic-category rendering verified end-to-end: the regenerated multi-agent example architecture renders Categories 1-8 findings AND Categories 9-10 findings adjacent in the same `category: agentic` section of `threats.md` and `threat-report.md`. The ID prefix `AG-{N}` is single-namespace with sequential numbering across all 10 categories.
- **SC-020**: PR title is `feat(219): asi07 inter-agent communication tool-abuse enrichment` (or similar Conventional Commit form ≤70 chars beginning with `feat(219):`). Pre-merge re-verification at `/aod.deliver` confirms the title; post-merge release-please PR opens within ~30s. If release-please skips, recovery via empty `feat(219): ... release marker` commit per F-212 incident precedent.
- **SC-021**: **Delivery retrospective filed** at `specs/219-asi07-tool-abuse-enrichment/delivery.md` (mirrors F-1 + F-2 precedent at `specs/201-...` and `specs/206-...`) capturing actual vs. estimated effort, **first execution of Heuristic A enrichment branch** lessons (precedent for F-6/F-7 Tier 2 ML+Mobile bundles), byte-identity preservation evidence (SC-006 + SC-010 grep proofs), and any deviations from PRD timeline or scope. Authored same-day-as-delivery if Day 1 PM merge has ≥1 hour residual capacity; otherwise authored on Buffer Day 1 (2026-04-29 Wednesday).

---

## Out of Scope (Explicit)

The following items are explicitly out of scope for F-3. Each carries a forward-reference per PRD scope boundaries:

1. **New `inter-agent-communication` agent** → explicitly REJECTED per Heuristic A consolidation; SDR-001 Decision 4 locked the resolution. Reopening this would re-adjudicate every prior Heuristic A consolidation (F-1 LLM05 → output-integrity, F-2 LLM09 → misinformation).
2. **Schema bump 1.7 → 1.8** → F-3 reuses the existing `AG-{N}` prefix; no new ID prefix introduced. F-3 is the first BLP-01 detection feature without a schema bump.
3. **`finding-format-shared.md` consumers list edit** → `tool-abuse` already at line 18 (PRD-time verified). No edit required.
4. **Functional orchestrator/dispatch-rules edits** → `tool-abuse` already fully registered (PRD-time verified). Only optional cosmetic annotation carve-out per PRD Q2 (architect plan-day decision).
5. **ASI06 (Intent Manipulation) enrichment** → different signal class; needs its own Heuristic A adjudication. Out-of-scope for F-3; future feature.
6. **ASI08 (Repudiation) enrichment** → STRIDE-tier with potential AG cross-cite; out-of-scope for BLP-01 Tier 1.
7. **MCP-04 / MCP-06 (additional MCP Top 10 items)** → existing `tool-abuse` already covers MCP-03 / MCP-05; remaining MCP items defer to a future MCP-bundle feature.
8. **Wiring all 24 detection-tier files to populate `source_attribution`** → F-A3 (separate Tier 1 follow-on; F-1 + F-2 + F-3 validate the contract on three independent producer flows).
9. **`trust-exploitation` agent for ASI09** → F-4 (separately unblocked via ADR-030 Decision 2 Outcome B).
10. **`denial-of-service` + `model-theft` enrichment for LLM10 Unbounded Consumption** → F-5 (parallel-eligible).
11. **OWASP ML Top 10 bundle** → F-6 Tier 2 (follow-on).
12. **OWASP Mobile Top 10 bundle** → F-7 Tier 2.
13. **Web/API attestation** → F-8 Tier 3.
14. **Cross-feature attack-chain reasoning** — Feature 141 cross-layer attack chains already covers this; new Category-9/10 findings flow through Phase 3.5 chain correlation automatically with zero F-3 scope.
15. **Section 4b "Findings by Agentic Pattern" entry** — F-3 findings emit `agentic_pattern` per orchestrator Phase 3.6; Section 4b adjustments are orchestrator-owned, not F-3 scope.
16. **Catalog population for additional MITRE ATLAS or CWE IDs** — F-3 cites only IDs verified present at PRD time (ASI07, CWE-287, CWE-345, AML.T0060, LLM03). Future catalog-enrichment features may upgrade additional adjacent citations.
17. **Commercial framing in ADR-032** — Layer 2, tachi Cloud, enterprise-only features explicitly excluded per public-ADR governance (Option C contract from SDR-001). Public ADR stands on technical merits only.

---

## Assumptions

- The architect confirms Heuristic A enrichment-vs-new-agent distinctness is intact (SDR-001 Decision 4 locked the resolution; ADR-030 + ADR-031 reinforced the signal-class taxonomy in the LLM tier; F-3 demonstrates the enrichment branch within the AG tier) at the start of Day 1 build and commits ADR-032 Proposed at the build-start schema-lock wave. If a subsume-into-new-agent signal surfaces during pattern authoring, escalation to architect is required before Day 1 EOD (per PRD R2 Day 1 gate).
- `examples/agentic-app/` is the regeneration target per PRD Q3 PM default. Architect may override at plan time with `examples/maestro-reference/` (canonical MAESTRO CDSS reference) or a newly-introduced minimal multi-agent fixture (consuming buffer day capacity) if `examples/agentic-app/` does not exhibit a sufficiently clean inter-agent channel signal post-Feature-142 extension.
- Pattern Category 9 indicator count is ≥4 per PRD FR-2 (PRD-time architect-leaning: 5 indicators); architect may add or refine indicators at plan time. Pattern Category 10 indicator count is ≥4 (PRD-time architect-leaning: 5 indicators); same plan-day refinement authority.
- Pattern Category 10 anti-indicator section ("single-MCP architectures emit zero Category-10 findings") MAY be made explicit in `detection-patterns.md` per PRD Q4 architect leaning YES — formalizes the multi-MCP topology gate for grep-auditability. Architect adjudicates final form at plan time.
- Cosmetic dispatch-rules annotation update (`tool-abuse (MCP-03)` → `tool-abuse (MCP-03, ASI-07)`) is contingent on PRD Q2 architect plan-day adjudication (architect leaning YES per PRD; documentation-only, no functional dispatch change).
- ADR-032 lands as Proposed at the start of the build phase to lock Heuristic A enrichment before pattern-catalog authoring; transitions to Accepted at PR merge with provisional merge-date and post-merge SHA fill (mirrors ADR-027/028/029/030/031 precedent). Plan stage finalizes the exact wave/slot.
- ADR-032 numbering is the next available number at merge time (PRD-time verified: ADR-031 is highest existing; ADR-032 is next). Team-lead reconfirms at plan time; renumber is a one-line edit if a parallel feature claims ADR-032 first.
- All 5 catalog citations (ASI07, CWE-287, CWE-345, AML.T0060, LLM03) are verified present at PRD time. Architect re-verifies at plan time per PRD R4. F-A2 referential-integrity validator passes by construction.
- F-A1, F-A2, F-B, F-1, F-2 dependencies are satisfied at PRD time (verified: `schemas/taxonomy/` populated with all 5 citations, `schemas/finding.yaml` v1.7 carries `source_attribution`, `tool-abuse` already populates source_attribution from prior wiring, `templates/tachi/security-report/coverage-attestation.typ` exists, `.claude/agents/tachi/output-integrity.md` and `.claude/agents/tachi/misinformation.md` present, ADR-030 + ADR-031 Accepted).
- PM + Architect + Team-Lead PRD sign-offs are in place (APPROVED / APPROVED_WITH_CONCERNS / APPROVED_WITH_CONCERNS per PRD frontmatter). All MEDIUM-1, MEDIUM-2 architect concerns and HIGH-1, HIGH-2, MEDIUM-1, MEDIUM-4 team-lead concerns were resolved inline in the PRD before sign-off (single-category resolution for Q1; Pattern Category Disambiguation paragraph in FR-2; Buffer Day 1 retrospective slot + DoD bullet 12; Release Discipline subsection + R6 risk; Q5 build-day calendar resolution; SC-10 wording softening for Q2).
- F-3 ships solo or as the first of multiple parallel-eligible Tier 1 features in the 2026-04-28 → 2026-04-30 window (parallel-eligible with F-4 and F-5 per PRD §BLP-01 Initiative Fit; F-3 ships first due to smallest edit surface). If F-4 or F-5 enters build concurrently, team-lead enforces sequencing on `tool-abuse.md` to avoid additive-edit conflicts. Buffer Day 2 (2026-04-30) absorbs concurrency-rebase work.
- Buffer Day 1 (2026-04-29 Wednesday) is reserved primarily for PRD R1 (regeneration friction on the chosen multi-agent example baseline) with contingent use for ADR-032 Accepted-transition polish or PR-review feedback turnaround. Delivery retrospective (DoD bullet 12) is filed Day 1 PM if merge completes with ≥1 hour residual capacity; otherwise filed on Buffer Day 1 as the primary buffer-day activity.
- Existing `tool-abuse` Categories 1-8 findings already populate `source_attribution` from prior wiring (PRD claim at FR-6 / FR-20). F-3 extends an existing populator with two new categories rather than adding a brand-new producer agent. This validates the F-A2 contract on enrichment in addition to the new-agent validation already established by F-1 + F-2.
- The PR title is set to `feat(219): asi07 inter-agent communication tool-abuse enrichment` (or similar Conventional Commit form ≤70 chars) at draft PR creation per PRD Release Discipline subsection. Title is re-verified pre-merge at `/aod.deliver` and recovery via empty release-marker commit is invoked if release-please skips post-merge per F-212 precedent.
